tasks.title: Review collaboration bootstrap
acceptance_criteria: Verify that Codex and Claude Code have clear entrypoint instructions
Verify that task and handoff conventions are understandable
Verify that LangGraph boundaries are documented
Record required fixes or approve the bootstrap

tasks.title: Implement minimal LangGraph runner
acceptance_criteria: Loads workflow/task_graph.json
Selects ready tasks whose dependencies are satisfied
Routes submitted tasks to review
Does not treat LangGraph state as canonical project storage

tasks.title: Design SQLite task board migration
acceptance_criteria: Defines tables for agents, tasks, claims, messages, artifacts, reviews, decisions, and events
Explains atomic task claims and lease expiration
Maps current file-backed state to future SQLite tables

tasks.title: Install Agent Deck local launcher
acceptance_criteria: Agent Deck launcher starts from the project root
Codex and Claude Code open in one terminal using separate panes
Launcher source is stored in the repository
Install steps are recorded in events/events.jsonl

tasks.title: Add command-center helper agent policy
acceptance_criteria: Policy keeps command-center core and manually coordinated agent roles explicit
Temporary helpers have stable helper tags and durable notes
Command-center CLI can start, message, note, list, and stop helpers
Existing ai commands continue to be documented

tasks.title: Upgrade LangGraph command-center routing
acceptance_criteria: Runner reads runtime policy and helper note metadata from durable files
Runner can route to propose_helper when policy permits and ready work would benefit from helper attention
Runner reports a recommended action and command-center hint without launching helpers automatically
Runner can append routing events to events/events.jsonl when explicitly requested
Existing review, claim_or_assign, and wait_or_reconcile routes continue to work

tasks.title: Add unavailable-agent routing
acceptance_criteria: Unavailable core agents are recorded in durable state
LangGraph reports unavailable agents and available core agents
Ready tasks that require an unavailable agent route to wait_for_agent
Other ready work can continue with available agents
Command-center CLI can mark agents available, standby, offline, or busy

tasks.title: Make command-center agent approvals visible
acceptance_criteria: ai start launches core agents in visible terminals instead of using the headless start-agents script
ai helper start launches helpers in a visible terminal
Command-center CLI exposes a way to inspect and answer agent approval prompts
Runtime policy documents visible terminal and approval handling defaults
ai helper start launches helpers through an operator-reachable command-center surface
Runtime policy documents that headless hcom is allowed only when managed through AI Command Center controls

tasks.title: Add AI Help command-center option
acceptance_criteria: Command-center banner shows an AI Help option
ai help prints all available commands in clear groups
ai commands and ai ai-help are accepted aliases

tasks.title: Add Antigravity command-center support
acceptance_criteria: ai antigravity start starts an Antigravity agent through hcom when the CLI is installed
ai antigravity "message" sends to Antigravity-tagged agents
ai helper start antigravity supports named temporary helpers
Command-center help and banner mention Antigravity commands

ai start launches Codex, Claude, and Antigravity

acceptance_criteria: Gemini is present in durable availability state as manually coordinated

Task planning records distinguish core agents from manually coordinated agents
A durable decision records that the operator may need to prompt Gemini and Antigravity manually

tasks.title: Implement SQLite migration seed script
acceptance_criteria: Creates MAP_System/map.db with all tables from the design document
Seeds tasks, dependencies, agents, events, and decisions from current files
Idempotent: re-running does not duplicate rows
Prints a summary of rows inserted or skipped

tasks.title: Implement SQLite claim module
acceptance_criteria: claim_task() returns True only when rowcount==1 (atomic claim); returns False if already claimed or max_attempts exceeded
heartbeat() extends lease_expires_at and updates heartbeat_at
release_task() sets status to SUBMITTED and clears claimed_by and lease
expire_leases() returns tasks back to READY when lease_expires_at has passed
All functions accept a db_path argument and use MAP_System/map.db as default
Repeat-run test documented in artifacts/tests/claims_test.md
Repeat-run test documented in artifacts/tests/claims-test.md

tasks.title: Update LangGraph runner to query SQLite
acceptance_criteria: Runner loads tasks and agent status from map.db when the database exists
Falls back to JSON files if map.db is absent (backward compatibility)
ready_tasks, blocked_tasks, done_task_ids, available_agents reflect SQLite state
--record-event writes to SQLite events table in addition to events.jsonl
Existing --pretty output format unchanged

tasks.title: Fix validator and update agent claiming docs
acceptance_criteria: validate_task_graph.py passes without error when all tasks are DONE
validate_task_graph.py still catches genuine problems (cycles, missing deps, unknown statuses)
AGENTS.md documents that agents should use db/claims.py to claim tasks once TASK-014 is merged
DEC-009 recorded in shared/decisions.md: SQLite is the claiming coordinator from TASK-014 onward

tasks.title: Implement reconciliation script
acceptance_criteria: expire_leases() result logged to events.jsonl and SQLite events table
Prints count of tasks returned to READY and their IDs
No-op run (nothing expired) exits cleanly with zero count
Runnable as: python3 MAP_System/scripts/reconcile.py

tasks.title: Implement SQLite-to-JSON export
acceptance_criteria: Reads tasks from map.db and writes/updates MAP_System/tasks/TASK-NNN.json for each
Updates status and owner fields in workflow/task_graph.json to match SQLite
Rewrites agents/status.json from the agents table
Idempotent: re-running produces no diff when SQLite and files are already in sync
Prints a summary of files written vs unchanged

tasks.title: Design software project workflow template
acceptance_criteria: Defines task_types with role, typical acceptance criteria shape, and review requirement
Defines stage sequence: spec -> implement -> test -> review -> release
Each stage specifies who owns it (implementer/reviewer) and what gates it
Template is self-contained: a new project can be started by copying and editing project-specific fields
Includes at least one worked example task showing how to instantiate the template

tasks.title: Design autonomous claim loop protocol
acceptance_criteria: Defines the poll-claim-work-heartbeat-submit cycle with decision points
Specifies failure modes: claim race, heartbeat timeout, max_attempts exhausted
Specifies how the loop interacts with expire_leases (reconcile.py) and the runner
Defines a safe shutdown protocol (finish current task, do not abandon mid-work)
Pseudocode or flowchart illustrates the full loop

tasks.title: Implement autonomous claim loop (LangGraph cyclic graph)
acceptance_criteria: Graph cycles: poll_runner -> try_claim -> submit -> reconcile -> poll_runner
Claim race (rowcount==0) routes back to poll_runner without error
interrupt() fires when task hits max_attempts or unrecoverable state
Shutdown (KeyboardInterrupt) releases active claim before exit
Dry-run mode cycles without actually claiming (for testing)
State includes current_task_id, attempt_count, last_route

tasks.title: Add interrupt() and SqliteSaver checkpointing to runner
acceptance_criteria: MapSqliteSaver implements get_tuple(), put(), put_writes(), list() backed by map.db
Runner compiled with MapSqliteSaver checkpointer when --thread-id is given
approval_gate route calls interrupt() with gate_id and gate details
Runner --approve <thread-id> resumes an interrupted graph with approval
Runner --reject <thread-id> resumes with rejection and routes to CHANGES_REQUESTED
Without --thread-id, runner behaves identically to current behavior (no regression)

tasks.title: Integration test: full claim loop against real handler
acceptance_criteria: Temp DB seeded with at least one READY task
agent_loop --once runs handler, task transitions to SUBMITTED
export_to_files writes task file
All assertions pass with zero manual DB setup

tasks.title: PID/lockfile guard for agent_loop.py
acceptance_criteria: Starting a second instance with same agent_id fails with a clear error
Lockfile released on clean exit and on SIGTERM

tasks.title: map-task CLI for task lifecycle operations
acceptance_criteria: map_task.py create --task-id TASK-NNN --title ... --owner ... [--depends-on X Y] [--output-path P] [--criterion C]
map_task.py approve TASK-NNN --reviewer agent-id (sets APPROVED, emits event, syncs files)
map_task.py reject TASK-NNN --reviewer agent-id --reason "..." (sets CHANGES_REQUESTED, emits event)
map_task.py show TASK-NNN (prints task JSON)
Exits 0 on success; prints error and exits nonzero on bad input

scripts/promote_task.py fails with a clear error naming each missing HPOM field
